description: Sector overlay for French technology companies, SaaS platforms, cloud services, internet intermediaries, software providers, data platforms, cybersecurity, and AI/digital-service documentation.

metadata.scope: France-only sector overlay controls for Technology. Intended to be used with france_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official French/EU legal, regulatory, supervisory, and guidance sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Arcom digital-content and platform governance materials

rules.title: Privacy-by-design and personal-data protection evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for privacy by design, GDPR/RGPD, lawful basis, consent, processor controls, DPO, or data-subject rights evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this sector control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Cybersecurity governance or incident-response evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for access control, encryption, logging, vulnerability management, incident response, backup, or ANSSI-aligned cybersecurity controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this sector control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Cloud, hosting, or third-party processing control evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for hosting, cloud provider, data localization/hosting, subcontractor, security certification, or service-level evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this sector control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Digital consumer terms, claims, or complaints evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for terms of service, pricing, subscription, complaints, withdrawal rights, claims, or consumer redress evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this sector control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Online-content moderation or reporting evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for moderation, reporting, content removal, illegal content, transparency, or platform governance evidence.
